project_goal: Build a lean, local-first AI project control cockpit that reduces owner babysitting.

owner_decisions.decision: Reducing owner babysitting is the primary rule.
Project Control Cockpit is local-first by default.
Project Control Cockpit V1 should not depend on direct paid API calls.
Claude Code is the initial worker layer for V1.
Advisor / verifier is a first-class role.
Worker claims are evidence, not truth.
The canonical project name is Project Control Cockpit. PCC is the approved short form.
Claude Code is the primary worker execution layer for PCC V1. Codex serves as the advisor/verifier and source-of-truth guard.
PCC must use explicit repo governance for canonical docs, idea intake, task promotion, and stale-doc handling. Ideas remain separate from active tasks.
Every meaningful change must trigger review and update of the relevant truth surfaces.
State consistency must be checked with a local deterministic validator before state updates are treated as complete.
A verified PASS advances the active task directly to complete rather than to an intermediate verified_pass state.
Worker-facing standing truth used by directive generation must live in canonical repo state rather than as hidden hardcoded script facts.
Fresh advisor and worker sessions must be restart-safe from canonical repo truth so chats are disposable rather than critical state containers.

Claude Code may temporarily hold both the worker and advisor/verifier roles, as an explicit, disclosed, owner-approved exception to DECISION-012, with independent re-checking and honest FAIL/INSUFFICIENT willingness required.

Verifier close-out includes running doctor/validate-cockpit-state, refreshing the advisor brief if stale, archiving cycle artifacts, and committing verified work to git. DECISION-065 later superseded the old per-push approval clause: a successful -Commit now pushes automatically under standing owner authorization.
V1 is complete as of pcc-v1-015; the project moves into BRR (Babysitter-Reduced Role) Phase 1, with reducing owner babysitting as the named top priority.

The BRR program plan is recorded canonically at docs/BRR_PLAN.md; Phase 1 is structured as four bounded, docs-only pcc-brr1 policy tasks (Owner Review Matrix, Task Safety Classification, Stop-Instead-of-Guess Policy, Operating Definitions), recommended for independent secondary review.
PCC returns to the canonical two-role split of DECISION-012: Claude Code is worker, Codex is advisor/verifier. The temporary dual-role trial (DECISION-019) ends and is superseded.
BRR Phase 1 policy scope is complete; PCC moves into BRR Phase 2, beginning with lightweight Task Classification Fielding as pcc-brr2-001.

Codex is available again. The DECISION-033 degraded self-verification fallback ends per its own terms; PCC returns to the canonical two-role split of DECISION-012/DECISION-023 (Claude Code worker, Codex advisor/verifier). A file-based polling bridge between the two is being scoped as a separate task.

BRR Phase 5 is closed and BRR's original goal (DECISION-001/DECISION-021) is declared satisfied, now that independent Codex verification (the single largest standing risk named in the Phase 5 Readiness Review) has been fielded and proven fully unattended end-to-end (pcc-brr5-004/pcc-brr5-005). PCC moves to post-BRR work. Four items (Class A self-accept unexercised, chaining beyond two cycles untested, fuller BRR Metrics undelivered, no formalized Failure Review Loop) are carried forward as non-blocking backlog. All standing BRR-era policy remains active.
Plain language is the standing default for user-facing explanations. Repo/process terms must be translated immediately rather than left as internal jargon.
The bounded extractability audit passes. PCC has reached the Maturity Checkpoint and is sound enough to freeze, back up, and branch from.
PCC has now properly reached the Maturity Checkpoint on the completed and independently verified pcc-pathC-004 audit cycle. The kernel is ready to freeze, back up, and branch from.

LLM use is minimized to irreducible judgment/verification. PCC's own tool runtime (scripts, dashboard) must be 100% local-deterministic with zero LLM dependency; task execution uses local shell tools by default, with an LLM only for the judgment portion a script cannot do; verification is the one structurally unavoidable LLM use, treated as the deliberately minimized exception.

Owner explicitly authorizes Path A / Category D Phase D3 (write-path controls) to begin, starting with pcc-pathD-007. The dashboard may now drop request files into a defined inbox that existing scripts/watchers act on; it still never executes work directly or edits authoritative state directly.

active_constraints: Favor modularity and extractability: every PCC capability must stay a clearly bounded unit over the .cockpit file bridge with no hidden shared state or undocumented cross-script assumptions.
Every PCC capability must use a documented .cockpit file-bridge contract with no hidden shared state or undocumented cross-script assumptions.
State updates require verifier PASS or explicit owner override.
Prefer local deterministic tools before model usage.
Avoid fake intelligence scoring and fake truth detection.
worker_context_facts: Worker claims are evidence, not truth.
Claude Code is ready and pointed at this repository workspace.
PCC owns the worker handoff contract through repo files; the owner should not need to restate the instructions manually.
When explaining repo state, workflow, or decisions, use plain language first and translate any necessary jargon immediately.
next_expected_action: Path A's currently-named Category D scope (pcc-pathD-001..009) is complete and verified, but the original project scope (archive/PCC Original Project Scope.md) is NOT fully built out (DECISION-101). The next task to draft is IDEA-015 (backlog/IDEAS.md): log routing decisions to routing-log.jsonl, closing the section 7.22 gap found in DECISION-101's audit. Owner has directed that PCC continue working rather than stop, since the original scope is not fully delivered.
